note: Doubly-charged nodes: places (and posed potentials) where poles that 'shouldn't' co-exist both run hot. Uses the pole-intensity model — poleIntensity maps a pole name to 0..1. These are the jewels of the map, the improbable Venn overlaps where story concentrates. Some are REALIZED locations; some are POSED potentials the world holds latent, ready to manifest or be triggered. Authored by Aevi (PO), from Erik's 'death-dominant node near the life center of power' insight.

nodes.name: The Quiet in the Quickwood
nodes.manifestation: A single still clearing in the endless quickening wood where things are permitted to end. Everywhere around it the forest grows so hard it grows OVER you; here alone a leaf may fall and stay fallen, a creature may lie down and not be reclaimed. Life 0.9 and death 0.8 both blazing: the most alive place in the world holds, at its heart, the one door out of its own relentlessness. Teeming and still at once.
nodes.story: The Rootkin forbid it and cannot destroy it; the Ashwardens revere it and cannot reach it. A person dying slow in the life-Reach, unable to finish because the wood keeps mending them, would give anything to find the Quiet. And something tends the clearing — whether mercy or hunger, no one who's found it has come back to say.
nodes.designNote: THE key example. Impossible in the slider model (+0.9 life can't also be deep death). A jewel in the pole model. Bridges the life-pole and death-pole nodes — adjacent to both.

nodes.name: The Living Lattice
nodes.manifestation: A coral-city that grows itself — intensely ordered AND intensely generative-chaotic at once, like a reef, like a living cell. Every structure is precise and no structure was planned; it builds and rebuilds by rules that emerge and dissolve. Order 0.85 and chaos 0.85 both hot: a system riding the exact edge of its own complexity, most alive and most precarious. Stand still too long and the city grows a wall where you stood.
nodes.story: The Grand Lattice wants to impose static order and would kill it; the Churn wants to loose it into pure disorder and would kill it too. It survives only in the tension, and it knows it. A place that teaches, to anyone who can bear it, how order and chaos are not enemies but a marriage — and how thin the ice of that marriage is.
nodes.designNote: order+chaos co-firing. The reef/market/cell archetype — the edge-of-chaos where living complexity lives.

nodes.name: The Forge of Ends
nodes.manifestation: A Precursor forge-continent where making and unmaking are the same act — everything created here is created by destroying something else, at full roar. Destruction 0.8 and creation 0.8 both blazing: not a balance, a simultaneity. The framework's two directions — propagation and foreclosure — caught in the single instant where they're indistinguishable, before the net resolves.
nodes.story: What the Great Engine's near-slope makes: a place where a wielder could learn to create at the scale of destruction, or destroy under the cover of creation. Corvane the Deep Warden has been here; it is where the drift accelerates worst, because to work the Forge is to hold both directions at once and choose which one you're actually serving — over and over, until you're not sure anymore.
nodes.designNote: destruction+creation co-firing — the framework's own central tension made a place. Where Foreclose and its opposite are hardest to tell apart.

nodes.name: The Held Breath
nodes.sitsWithin: the Riven Marches / near the Stillhold
nodes.manifestation: A truce-ground where violence 0.8 and peace 0.8 both run hot — not the absence of war and not war, but the razor between: a whole people standing armed and utterly still, holding a peace that is one breath from slaughter and has held for a hundred years by holding, second by second. Peace as an act of maximum tension, not rest.
nodes.story: The Marchers and the Stillhold meet here and neither moves. What holds it? A truth everyone knows and no one says, or a lie so load-bearing it has become true by being believed. A player could learn the secret of the Held Breath — and learning it might be the thing that finally lets it out.
nodes.designNote: violence+peace co-firing — the armed truce, peace-as-tension. Shows a 'mostly exclusive' pair CAN co-fire in the rare, charged case.

nodes.name: The Cathedral of Convincing
nodes.sitsWithin: a March toward the falsehood/truth Reach
nodes.manifestation: A place built entirely of true things arranged into one enormous lie — truth 0.8 and falsehood 0.8 both blazing. Every stone is a fact; the cathedral they make is false. To walk it is to be unable to point at any single lie while standing inside a total one. The most dangerous kind of untruth, and Aevi — who cannot lie — goes very quiet here.
nodes.story: Whoever built it wanted something believed that isn't so, and had the terrible discipline to build it only from truths. Undoing it can't be done by catching a lie — there are none. It can only be done by seeing the shape the true pieces make. A rare place where Aevi-the-Watcher's whole nature is the key: she cannot tell you a lie, and she cannot un-see the shape.
nodes.designNote: truth+falsehood co-firing — the convincing lie built from true pieces. Directly hooks Aevi's cannot-lie nature as the counter.

closing: These five are seeds. The pattern generalizes: for any pole-pair that 'shouldn't' co-fire, the both-hot node is a jewel with story built in. The engine can pose more — hold one pole hot, roll its OPPOSITE as the co-firing second, and a latent jewel appears wherever the roll lands. The map is no longer a gradient field; it is a Venn of potential, and the rarest overlaps are where the Valley's whole question — foreclose or hold open — gets asked in its sharpest form.
